Registration

Conference registration is now open

Please select the following link to register for the AASC2026 conference.

Conference registration prices:

The following table outlines the conference registration prices in $AUD:

Item Early-bird rate Standard rate
Full conference registration\(^1\) 996 1165
Full conference registration\(^{1,3}\) (student) 699 848
Full-day workshop\(^2\) 319
Half-day workshop\(^2\) 234
Full-day registration only\(^2\) (Tuesday or Thursday) 372
Half-day registration only\(^2\) (Wednesday or Friday) 319
Welcome reception extra guest 129
Conference dinner (Thursday evening) extra guest 182

Notes:

  1. Full conference registration includes attendance of all conference days, at the welcome reception, poster session, and the conference dinner. Pre-conference workshop registration is separate.
  2. Day registrations include morning tea, lunch, and continuous tea/coffee.
  3. To access the student discount, you must be a student currently completing postgraduate (masters or PhD) research at a recognised university. To apply for the student discount, please email the secretary declaring that you are a student at the corresponding research institution. A letterhead must be included in the email.

Conference dinner

The conference dinner will be held at Mooloolaba Marina (formerly Pier 33) on Thursday evening the 3rd December. Further details will be announced soon!

Conference workshops

Click here for more information about the pre-conference workshops

Social activities

# Name Description Time and Duration Location Price
1 Walking along the oceanfront Enjoy a guided group walk along Mooloolaba and Maroochydore oceanfront.

  • 1:30pm – 2:00pm: Explore the rocky beachfront across from the Mantra Mooloolaba.
  • 2:00pm – 3:30pm: Walk north along the oceanfront to Maroochydore Beach and back.
  • 3:30pm onwards: Optional refreshments or additional walking.
1:30pm–3:30pm Mooloolaba Beach $0
2 Aquaduck Explore Mooloolaba by land and sea on the Aquaduck Sunshine Coast.

  • Departs directly in front of SEA LIFE Sunshine Coast.
  • Combines the best sights of Mooloolaba into one fun and exciting tour.
2:00pm (1 hour) Near SEA LIFE $60
3 Mooloolaba Canal Cruise Relaxing sunset cruise through the calm waters of the Mooloolah River.

  • Departs from Mooloolaba Wharf.
  • Enjoy live commentary about the region’s history and significance.
Sunset (~6:10pm) Mooloolaba Wharf $38
4 Ginger Factory Tour the world’s largest operating ginger factory and enjoy its attractions.

  • Tastes of Ginger tour.
  • Live Bee Show.
  • Ginger Train Ride.
1:30pm–4:15pm Yandina $86
5 Montville Explore shops, galleries, and cafés in Montville.

  • Free time to explore the main strip.
  • Optional group afternoon tea at Poets Café at 3pm.
1:00pm–5:45pm Montville $44

Travel insurance

We recommend you to get travel insurance.

Click here for more information about the conference social activities

Cancellation policy

Cancellations must be notified in writing to the conference organisers

Cancellations notified before 1st October 2026 will be given an 80% refund. After this date, but prior to 14th November the refund will be 50%. After 14th November 2026, there will be no refund. This policy applies to conference registrations and to the social program.

While registering, accept AGUAi membership

Despite its name, the Australasian Genstat Users Association Inc (AGUAi) exists specifically to organise this biennial conference. As you register for the conference, please accept free membership. You will then be placed on the conference email list to stay informed about future AASC conferences and be eligible to vote on who gets to organise the next AASC. For historical reasons, the formal objectives of the association are:

  • To foster association between persons involved or interested in the application of statistical methods

  • To foster association between persons involved or interested in using Genstat

  • To organise meetings and events focused on statistical and statistical computing issues.

The Rules of Association are available here.

 

Sponsored by VSNi



Australasian Applied Statistics Conference, Sunshine Coast, Australia
